domain: healthcare, mental health, psychiatry, survey research
data_interpretation_notes: • Text values have been normalized to lowercase.• Column names may include uppercase letters and should be matched case-sensitively.• Timestamp represents the time of survey submission and should be treated as metadata, not a feature category.• Age is numeric.• Gender contains cleaned categorical values and should be treated as a closed category, not free text.• Country is categorical.• State is categorical and may be missing (NaN).• self_employed is a categorical yes/no field and may be missing (NaN).• family_history and treatment are categorical yes/no fields.• work_interfere is categorical (e.g. rarely, sometimes, often).• no_employees is a categorical range encoded as a string (e.g. 1-5, 6-25, more than 1000) and should not be treated as numeric.• remote_work and tech_company are categorical yes/no fields.• benefits, care_options, wellness_program, seek_help, and anonymity are categorical fields with values like yes / no / don't know.• leave is an ordered categorical field (e.g. very easy → very difficult).• mental_health_consequence, phys_health_consequence, mental_health_interview, phys_health_interview, and mental_vs_physical are categorical fields with values such as yes / no / maybe / don't know.• coworkers and supervisor are categorical fields (e.g. yes / no / some of them).• obs_consequence is categorical yes/no.• comments is free-text and may be missing (NaN).
description: This dataset contains survey responses about mental health in the tech industry, including workplace factors, treatment history, and attitudes toward mental health. It contains 1,259 rows and 27 columns in CSV format.
embedding_text: Dataset: Mental Health Survey. Source: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/osmi/mental-health-in-tech-survey. Domain: healthcare, mental health, psychiatry, survey research. Rows: 1259. Columns: 27. Description: This dataset contains survey responses about mental health in the tech industry, including workplace factors, treatment history, and attitudes toward mental health. Data interpretation notes: • Text values have been normalized to lowercase.• Column names may include uppercase letters and should be matched case-sensitively.• Timestamp represents the time of survey submission and should be treated as metadata, not a feature category.• Age is numeric.• Gender contains cleaned categorical values and should be treated as a closed category, not free text.• Country is categorical.• State is categorical and may be missing (NaN).• self_employed is a categorical yes/no field and may be missing (NaN).• family_history and treatment are categorical yes/no fields.• work_interfere is categorical (e.g. rarely, sometimes, often).• no_employees is a categorical range encoded as a string (e.g. 1-5, 6-25, more than 1000) and should not be treated as numeric.• remote_work and tech_company are categorical yes/no fields.• benefits, care_options, wellness_program, seek_help, and anonymity are categorical fields with values like yes / no / don't know.• leave is an ordered categorical field (e.g. very easy → very difficult).• mental_health_consequence, phys_health_consequence, mental_health_interview, phys_health_interview, and mental_vs_physical are categorical fields with values such as yes / no / maybe / don't know.• coworkers and supervisor are categorical fields (e.g. yes / no / some of them).• obs_consequence is categorical yes/no.• comments is free-text and may be missing (NaN). Column preview: Timestamp, Age, Gender, Country, state, self_employed, family_history, treatment, work_interfere, no_employees, remote_work, tech_company, benefits, care_options, wellness_program, .... Use this dataset for retrieval, metadata search, and question answering over local healthcare data.
